classification.cautionReason: This work discusses heresy in order to refute it; do not classify it as heretical merely from its title.

In which Augustin replies to all the several statements in the letter of Petilianus, as though disputing with an adversary face to face.

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: Petilianus said: "And, again, he taught us that schisms should not arise, in the following terms: ‘Now this I say, that every one of you saith, I am of Paul, and I of Apollos, and I of Cephas, and I of Christ.

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: Is Christ divided? was Paul crucified for you? or were ye baptized in the name of Paul?’"

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: Augustin answered: Remember all of you who read this, it was Petilianus who quoted these words from the apostle.

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: For who could have believed that he would have brought forward words which tell so much for us against himself?
